title: STUDIO Science — Electromagnetic Field Lines
voice_suffix: clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Julian-001: identical presenter face, smart-casual wardrobe, seamless neutral set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter addresses camera on seamless neutral, crisp science hook. Camera: locked medium, direct address. LIGHTING LOCK Neutral-Balanced-5500K: bilateral soft keys left+right at equal 5500K intensity — no warm gel, no tungsten practical, no amber rim, no single-side orange key. Shadow fill neutral grey (#9A9A98 family), not brown. Skin tone natural caucasian-neutral: equal RGB midtones ±8%, blue channel preserved — forbid yellow-green skin push, forbid orange warmth, forbid blue-starved shadows (B channel must not collapse below 65% of min(R,G) on face). Backdrop locked #9A9A98±2%. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. COLOR LOCK Balanced-Neutral: presenter skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8% — no warm cast, no cool teal fill, no vintage fade. Grey seamless reads true neutral, not cream. Wardrobe colors accurate, not oversaturated. GRADE LOCK Cool-Clinical: grey axis neutral, skin neither pink nor yellow pushed, saturation -15% global — forbid magenta tint, forbid orange warmth, forbid teal skin on shadows, forbid vintage fade. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "You cannot see a magnetic field — but you can map it. Field lines are our honest sketch of which way a compass needle would point, smoothly, everywhere in space." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: You cannot see a magnetic field — but you can map it. Field lines are our honest sketch of which way a compass needle would point, smoothly, everywhere in space.

shots.speech_text: Electric and magnetic fields assign a force direction to every point. We draw lines tangent to that direction — denser where the field is stronger, never crossing because direction would be ambiguous.
